subject: Chemistry
title: Mendeleev Predicts Missing Elements
description: One-minute skill: Mendeleev Predicts Missing Elements.

hook.text: In 1869, Mendeleev arranged 63 elements by weight and left gaps—predicting elements that hadn't been found yet.

buildup.text: He noticed that properties repeat periodically when elements are ordered by atomic weight.

discovery.text: He left gaps for three undiscovered elements, predicting their atomic weights and chemical properties.

twist.text: Within 15 years, all three were found: gallium, scandium, and germanium—matching his predictions almost exactly.

climax.text: The periodic table became chemistry's most powerful organizing tool—and Mendeleev its greatest prophet.

punchline.text: Gaps in a table that predicted the future. Pattern recognition at its finest.

quiz.question: How did Mendeleev validate his periodic table?
options: He predicted properties of undiscovered elements that were later found to match
He synthesized three new elements in his laboratory
He proved that all elements are made of the same atom
